channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sensex and Nifty Market Updates for 6 April with RBI Rate Decisions and Global Events
item.description: The stock market updates for 6 April provide an overview of key movements in Sensex and Nifty indices amidst ongoing domestic and global economic developments. Investors were closely monitoring the Re...

item.title: Mother of Kakamega Polytechnic Principal Says Her Daughter Faced Threats Before Death
item.description: The late Carolyne Khamete, a principal at Kakamega County Polytechnic, was found dead on the shores of Lake Victoria after missing for more than a week. Her mother revealed that before her death, Kham...

item.title: Internet Bug Bounty Program Pauses Rewards Due to AI-Driven Increase in Vulnerability Reports
item.description: The Internet Bug Bounty program, which has been active since 2012 and funded by several leading software companies, announced a pause in new submissions. The program has historically rewarded research...

item.title: Savannah Guthrie Returns to Today Show Amid Ongoing Search for Her Mother
item.description: Savannah Guthrie, co-anchor of NBC's Today show, is returning to the program for the first time since her mother, Nancy Guthrie, went missing over two months ago. Nancy, 84, disappeared from her Arizo...

item.title: Key Political, Security, and Economic Updates Across Africa
item.description: On 6th April 2026, multiple significant developments unfolded across Africa. In Cameroon, President Paul Biya plans to appoint a vice-president for the first time in over 43 years following parliament...

item.title: Paramount seeks Gulf sovereign fund support for Warner Bros acquisition
item.description: Paramount Skydance is negotiating to secure around $24 billion (R404.24 billion) in equity commitments from three Gulf sovereign wealth funds, led by Saudi Arabia’s Public Investment Fund (PIF), to su...

item.title: China’s Long-Term Energy Strategy Softens the Blow of Middle East Conflict
item.description: The New York Times reports that while the war in the Middle East has triggered a major energy shock, China — the world’s largest oil buyer — has shown notable resilience thanks to long-term strategic ...

item.title: Bengali Film and TV Industry Halts Production After Actor Rahul Banerjee’s Fatal Accident
item.description: The Bengali film and television industry has come to a complete standstill following the sudden and tragic death of actor Rahul Banerjee during a shoot. On April 6, 2026, several prominent association...

item.title: NDP's Avi Lewis Election Win Raises Questions About Party Direction and Representation
item.description: The New Democratic Party (NDP) recently elected Avi Lewis as its leader, a victory that has ignited a significant debate within the party and across the Canadian political landscape. Lewis, a candidat...

item.title: Grieving Mother Places Cutlass in Slain Son's Casket During Burial in Nigeria
item.description: In a deeply emotional and dramatic burial ceremony, a grieving mother from Nigeria opened her slain son's casket and placed a cutlass inside it while chanting instructions to the deceased. The heartbr...

item.title: Trump reinstalls Columbus statue, sparking renewed debate over America’s historical legacy
item.description: In late March 2026, US President Donald Trump reinstated a statue of Christopher Columbus on the White House grounds, fulfilling his 2025 executive order to restore what he described as truth and sani...

item.title: Sean Hepburn Ferrer reflects on Audrey Hepburn’s life, legacy and humanity in his new biography
item.description: Sean Hepburn Ferrer, the son of iconic actress Audrey Hepburn, has released a new biography titled *Intimate Audrey*, offering a deeply personal glimpse into his mother’s life beyond her glamorous fil...

item.title: NDPC Launches Probe into Data Handling Practices of Remita and Sterling Bank
item.description: The Nigeria Data Protection Commission (NDPC) don begin serious investigation into claims say Remita Payment Services Ltd., Sterling Bank, plus other related organisations, fit don leak personal data....

item.title: Top 10 African Countries With the Highest IMF Debts in 2026
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has become a crucial source of financial recovery for many African countries in the aftermath of the COVID-19 pandemic. However, these loans are now placing a hea...

item.title: The Importance of Being Cautious About Fake News and Its Impact on Society
item.description: In today's digital age, the spread of fake news can have serious consequences on society. Fake news, often circulated via social media, misleads the public, fuels division, and damages trust in govern...

item.title: NHS Urges Patients to Attend Appointments During Six-Day Doctors' Strike
item.description: The NHS has urged patients in England not to delay seeking medical care as a six-day strike by resident doctors commences. This is the fifteenth walkout in a long-running dispute over pay and career p...

item.title: SC to Hear Imran Khan's Review Petition in Defamation Case on April 7
item.description: The Supreme Court of Pakistan is set to resume the hearing of a review petition filed by Imran Khan regarding the Rs10 billion defamation suit filed by Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif. The case, which h...

item.title: IHC Demands Report on Alleged Violation of Tree-Felling Ban by CDA
item.description: On April 6, 2026, the Islamabad High Court (IHC) directed the Capital Development Authority (CDA) to submit a report regarding allegations of disobeying a court order that restrains tree felling in Is...

item.title: Security Deployed at INEC Headquarters in Abuja Amid ADC Leadership Dispute
